quote: Your call has been forwarded to an automated voice messaging system.
stewart_read: The call never connected to a live customer. The entire transcript is the voicemail system's automated greeting. There is no rep action, no customer state, and no interaction to evaluate. This is a dead-dial record.
coaching_implication: No rep coaching is warranted from this transcript — there was no rep behavior to evaluate. The manager's action here is operational, not coaching: confirm whether the rep attempted a callback, logged the voicemail attempt in the CRM, and followed the floor's dead-dial protocol (attempt count, re-queue timing, whether a voicemail was left). If the rep is accumulating a pattern of dead-dial records without documented follow-up attempts, that is the coaching conversation — but it requires CRM data, not this transcript.

stewart_read: The voicemail system reads back the dialed number. This is the only 'data' in the transcript. It confirms the number was live enough to route to voicemail but not answered. No inference about customer intent, qualification, or interest is possible from this.
coaching_implication: If this number is a paid lead, the bill-collection requirement (context.lead_types_and_bill_collection_rules) means this lead cannot be booked until a live conversation occurs. The manager should verify the lead type and confirm the rep has a re-dial or follow-up queued. A paid lead that sits as a dead-dial without re-contact is a direct compensation miss under the stair-step model (context.setter_compensation_model).

stewart_read: The voicemail system confirms the number is not available. This is a no-contact outcome. Under Ion's compensation model, setters are paid on sits — a dead-dial produces zero value. The only question is whether the rep has a documented re-contact strategy.
coaching_implication: Manager should check: (1) Was a voicemail left? The transcript does not show one. (2) Was the lead re-queued for callback? (3) If this is a paid lead, the bill-collection gate means the sit cannot be booked without live contact — every dead-dial on a paid lead is a potential missed sit. If the rep is not leaving voicemails or re-queuing dead-dials, that is a floor-level process gap worth addressing in the 1-on-1.

quote: At the tone, please record your message. When you've finished recording, you may hang up, or press 1 for more options.
stewart_read: The transcript ends here. No voicemail was recorded in the transcript, no rep speech appears anywhere. Either the rep hung up without leaving a message, or the recording ended before capturing any rep action. Either way, there is no coaching content in this call record.
coaching_implication: If the rep routinely hangs up on voicemail without leaving a message, that is a recoverable process gap — a brief, scripted voicemail (name, Ion Solar, Tesla-certified installer framing, callback number) can generate inbound callbacks and costs the rep under 20 seconds. Manager should ask the rep directly: 'What do you do when you hit voicemail?' and calibrate from the answer.
